objective.development_speed: increase parallel useful throughput and reduce Codex rereading
objective.development_accuracy: increase first-pass correctness, evidence integrity, and defect detection
objective.development_quality: improve maintainability, runtime fidelity, security, performance, and product QA

implemented: signed task-intent admission ledger before substantive worker-eligible reasoning
independent concurrent Cursor and Claude scheduled lanes
plain gpt-5.3-codex guarded implementation in isolated worktrees
Sonnet 5 primary semantic routing and high-assurance preflight/residual-risk graph
DPAPI-protected HMAC request, control, index, monitor, adoption, and qualification records
priority, TTL, idempotency, dependencies, cancellation, supersession, bounded retry, stale-scope rejection, and dead letter
outer wrapper process-tree timeout supervision independent of wrapper self-reporting
credential-scrubbed host validator broker and post-validator scope verification
compact signed Codex review packets with diff hash/excerpt and validation results
local deterministic worker health and read-only EC2 safety monitors
automatic routing, latency, first-pass, dead-letter, weighted-adoption, scope, quality, and direct-usage qualification metrics

cursor_usage_baseline.interpretation: Observed Cursor usage exists, but the zero automation IDs confirm that prior infrastructure was not automatically feeding production work. Utilization alone does not prove Codex reduction.

qualification.reason: Production handoffs and matched Codex usage windows begin only after protected merge and live deployment.
qualification.required: 25 substantive handoffs plus completion, weighted adoption, scope, routing, first-pass, dead-letter, critical-defect, two five-hour, and two 24-hour/weekly-rate gates

codex_automation_migration.migration_gate: Apply only after protected merge, installed local services, and successful task canaries.
changes: pause combined worker monitor after local health replacement
EC2 Codex sentinel hourly to every six hours with local 15-minute read-only monitor
anti-loop supervisor every two hours to every six hours
milestone auditor every six hours to daily
stale automation cleanup every three hours to daily
